## Summary

Fresh rescue branch for PR #875, rebased onto current `main`, carrying only the scoped Phase 0.5/checklist/runtime work plus the minimal build/test fixes needed to keep that slice mergeable.

This branch preserves the intended #875 scope without reopening the stale divergence on `pr/865-seed-migration-scaffold`.

## Scope

In scope:

- Phase 0 authority proof and checklist runtime wiring.
- Phase 0.5A Story Map seed producer and Phase 0.5B Revise Opportunity seed producer.
- Worker/resume checklist-entry gate integration.
- Recommendation-format regression fix and focused smoke/test updates required for this branch to build and test cleanly.

Out of scope:

- Architecture redesign beyond the scoped rescue/replay of #875 work.
- New evaluation phases, new providers, or new runtime pipelines.
- Fresh product-scope expansion outside the checklist/seed/revise slice.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0
Head-SHA: 008788439c95a28f034be73eba5f3e4ccdc8463f

## Risks & Anomalies

- This PR is a rescue branch, so the main operational risk is accidental scope drift back into the stale predecessor branch.
- Checks must be read from the current PR head only; earlier failures on stale SHAs are not valid evidence against this rescue head.
- Pipeline Stress Harness may complete after the core CI/governance checks; it should be evaluated on the current head only.

## Unauthorized Input Sources

- None added.
- This branch continues to read only persisted manuscript/evaluation/job/ledger data already inside the authorized application boundary.

## Internal Process Leakage

- No internal-only reasoning, operator workflow, or hidden pipeline diagnostics are newly exposed in public UI copy.
- User-visible progress/status strings remain public-safe and operationally generic.

## Input → Action → Output

- Input: persisted job/manuscript/evaluation state plus existing revise/checklist artifacts.
- Action: enforce checklist entry rules, persist Phase 0 authority proof, materialize Phase 0.5 seed artifacts, and present revise/final-review surfaces over persisted state.
- Output: governed queue progression, seed artifacts, persisted recommendation/report output, and UI-safe status signals.

## Public-Safe Quality/Status Metrics

- Exposed status remains limited to public-safe job/progress/report states.
- No hidden provider metrics, token counts, or internal adjudication traces are surfaced to end users.

## Runtime/Pipeline Expansion

- No new external provider is introduced.
- No hidden background runtime path is added beyond the scoped Phase 0/0.5/checklist work already represented in this PR.
- The rescue branch narrows scope to the intended #875 slice rather than expanding the pipeline.

## Latency Impact

No-Pipeline-Impact: N/A — this PR changes governed evaluation runtime behavior, so latency must be evaluated as scoped functional change rather than claimed absent.

- No evidence in this rescue slice indicates an intentional latency increase beyond the existing governed evaluation path.
- Build/test fixes included here are narrow compatibility corrections, not runtime fan-out expansion.

## Evaluation Process Change Declaration

Process Change: yes

- [x] Sequential phase-gate doctrine preserved (parallelism only within safe sub-workloads).
- [x] Phase 0 remains first and is proven before downstream processing.
- [x] Phase 2 remains blocked on accepted_story_ledger_v1 (Review Gate authority).
- [x] Phase 3 remains blocked on pass12_handoff_v1 and is sole owner of Pass 3B synthesis.
- [x] Deterministic quality gates run after Pass 3B and before completion.
- [x] WAVE remains post-evaluation (after evaluation_result_v2) and non-fatal to base evaluation.

One-line doctrine: The pipeline is sequential at the phase/gate level and parallel only inside safe sub-workloads.

Process-Change Impact Summary (required when Process Change: yes):
- Persists Phase 0 authority proof and checklist enforcement before downstream progress.
- Adds Phase 0.5 Story Map and Revise Opportunity seed producers within the governed evaluation flow.
- Keeps worker/resume entry on the checklist-gated path instead of bypassing it.
- Preserves existing downstream phase ownership while making the #875 slice replayable from current `main`.

Pass Selection:
- [x] Pass 1
- [ ] Pass 2
- [ ] Pass 3

## Contract Integrity

- Job status vocabulary remains canonical (`queued`, `running`, `complete`, `failed`).
- Checklist entry gating and Phase 0 authority proof are enforced through persisted/runtime contracts, not inferred UI state.
- Rescue replay does not alter the governing contract for Review Gate, Pass 2 handoff, or Pass 3 synthesis ownership.

## Behavioral Quality

This PR is not reducing intelligence.

- The rescue branch preserves fail-closed checklist behavior.
- Recommendation formatting remains criteria-derived and human-readable after the targeted regression fix.
- Progress/report surfaces continue to read persisted state instead of guessing hidden runtime state.
